steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence identifies someone as ‘one of many nonpresidents’ and then needs a word that restricts which nonpresidents are meant: those who appear on US currency. The blank must function as a noun modifier in that position.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — appears: a finite present-tense verb cannot modify a noun directly. ‘nonpresidents appears on US currency’ is not grammatical in this structure. ✗
  • Choice B — to appear: an infinitive phrase functions as a restrictive modifier of a noun — equivalent to ‘that appear.’ ‘nonpresidents to appear on US currency’ correctly narrows which nonpresidents are being referenced. ✓
  • Choice C — had appeared: a finite past-perfect verb cannot serve as a noun modifier in this position. ✗
  • Choice D — did appear: a finite emphatic past verb cannot modify a noun directly. ✗
That is the correct answer.
